incorrects.description: Student selected 4 × 2: reversed the groups and items per group

params.description: Both boxes highlight to draw attention to counting the groups first.

steps.text: The first number tells you how many groups. Count the boxes first. How many boxes do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 + 4: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Boxes highlight to show equal groups structure.

steps.text: These are equal groups, not just numbers to add. The × symbol shows groups. Count the boxes, then look inside one box.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 + 2: used addition and reversed the values

params.description: Boxes highlight to emphasize equal groups structure.

steps.text: You need the × symbol for equal groups, not plus. Count the groups first, then count what's in each group.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Boxes highlight sequentially: 1, 2. Then one box zooms to show 4 items inside, counted 1, 2, 3, 4.

steps.text: Let me show you. First, I count the boxes. 1, 2. Two boxes. Those are the groups. Now I look inside one box: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four items in each group. So it's 2 groups of 4, which we write as 2 × 4.
